```markdown
# Transportation Impact Brief
## South Portland Elementary Reconfiguration — General Audience

---

## Executive Summary

The South Portland School Board is being asked to vote on an elementary school reconfiguration before its transportation analysis is complete. Depending on which option is chosen, the combined cost to the district and families ranges from $30,000 to $1.68 million per year — and under the district's own recommended option, that cost could equal or exceed the savings the reconfiguration is supposed to generate. This analysis does not exist yet in any board-facing document.

---

## Key Finding

**Every option on the table requires more bus drivers than the district currently employs — before any cuts to the driver pool.**

The district confirmed 20 drivers at the March 30, 2026 board meeting. The recommended Option A requires 30. The board is scheduled to vote before the transportation consultant has finished its work.

---

## Configuration Comparison

| | **Option A** (Admin Recommended) | **Option B** (K–4 Proximity) | **Variant C** (Citizen Proposal) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Drivers needed | 30 | 24 | 29 |
| Drivers on hand | 17–20 | 17–20 | 17–20 |
| Driver shortfall | 10–13 | 4–7 | 9–12 |
| Families split across buildings | 139–169 | 0 | 123–150 |
| District annual cost | $803K–$876K | $30K–$80K | $670K–$736K |
| Family annual cost | $144K–$804K | $0 | $127K–$714K |
| **Total annual fiscal exposure** | **$946K–$1.68M** | **$30K–$80K** | **$797K–$1.45M** |
| As % of claimed savings | 43%–112% | 1.4%–5.3% | 36%–97% |

*District costs include route expansion ($748K/yr for Option A, $624K/yr for Variant C, $0 for Option B). Family costs reflect estimated childcare gaps due to mismatched school schedules. Option B produces no split families and no route expansion.*

---

## What's Missing

The district has not produced — and the board has not seen — any of the following:

- **A complete route model** for any configuration. The Director of Operations stated at the March 30 meeting that routing will be operationalized after the vote.
- **A driver hiring and retention plan.** No analysis exists of how the district would close a shortfall of 4–13 drivers, or at what cost.
- **A childcare capacity audit.** After-school care is full at four of five existing schools. No study has been done of what happens to families displaced by schedule mismatches.
- **An explanation of the district's own cost spike.** South Portland's transportation costs rose 50.2% from FY23 to FY25, compared to a statewide average of 10.5%. No cause has been identified.
- **Substantiation for the "not significant" claim.** At the March 2 meeting, the administration stated it did not anticipate route changes being a significant cost — without any supporting analysis. This analysis estimates those costs at $624K–$748K per year.

---

## Limitations

This analysis uses FY25 cost baselines and enrollment estimates. Driver wages, diesel prices, and actual route geometry have not been independently verified. The range widths reflect genuine uncertainty, not a flaw in the method. The lower bounds assume favorable conditions; the upper bounds assume adverse ones. Real outcomes will fall somewhere in between.

---

## Invitation to Improve

The district can narrow these ranges significantly by releasing:

- The consultant's route models for each configuration
- Current SEA driver wage schedules
- After-school care enrollment and waitlist data by school
- The methodology behind the "not significant" cost claim

This brief is a placeholder for analysis the district has not yet produced. If the district releases that data before the vote, these figures should be updated.
```
